title: Listening - Part 4: Fitness After Fifty
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people over the age of fifty talk about starting a new fitness regime. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I took up swimming at sixty because [21]I’d developed some joint issues and my physiotherapist was quite insistent that I needed to stay mobile[/21]. I’ve never been a fan of the water, to be honest. But what I’ve found is that [26]it’s given me a huge amount of mental clarity[/26]. When I’m in the pool, I’m not thinking about my bills or my health; I’m just focused on my stroke. It’s like a form of meditation. || Speaker 2: I joined a walking football team. [22]I was feeling a bit down after I retired because I missed the camaraderie of the office[/22], and I needed a reason to get out of the house. It’s been brilliant. [27]I’ve actually lost quite a bit of weight[/27], which wasn't even my goal, but it’s a nice bonus. My clothes fit better and I have so much more energy to play with my grandkids now. || Speaker 3: I started yoga because [23]my daughter was constantly nagging me about my posture[/23]. She said I was starting to hunch over. It was a bit of an ego blow, so I went to prove her wrong. The best thing, though, has been [28]the quality of my sleep[/28]. I used to lie awake for hours, but now I’m out as soon as my head hits the pillow. It’s changed my whole mood during the day. || Speaker 4: I took up weightlifting at fifty-five. [24]I’d read some research about the importance of bone density in older women[/24], and I wanted to be proactive. I was a bit intimidated by the gym at first. But [29]I’ve gained a massive sense of empowerment[/29]. Knowing that I can lift things that men half my age struggle with is a great feeling. It’s definitely boosted my self-esteem. || Speaker 5: I started cycling because [25]I wanted to reduce my reliance on my car for short trips to the shops[/25]. I’m trying to be more eco-conscious. It’s been tough on the hills! But the real reward has been [30]the new friendships I’ve made[/30]. I’ve joined a local club for 'vintage' cyclists, and we have a laugh. It’s made me realise that getting older doesn't mean you have to be sedentary.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) why each speaker started their new fitness activity.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1's physiotherapist was 'insistent' because of joint issues.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 'missed the camaraderie' of work after retirement.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3's daughter was 'nagging' them about their posture.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 read 'research about the importance of bone density'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted to 'reduce reliance on my car' and be eco-conscious.

options.B: to replace a lost social connection
options.C: to prepare for a specific event
options.D: to raise money for a charity
options.F: to respond to a family member's concern

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker has found most rewarding.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 finds it gives them 'mental clarity' and acts like meditation.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 has 'lost quite a bit of weight' and has more energy.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3's 'quality of sleep' has improved significantly.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 feels a 'sense of empowerment' and boosted self-esteem.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 values the 'new friendships' they have made.

options.B: the chance to travel to new places
options.F: the satisfaction of beating a record
options.G: an improvement in their physical appearance
options.H: the expansion of their social circle
